Program_Description: WIC provides federally-funded temporary assistance for Women who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have recently had a baby and Infants and Children under five years of age.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children.
Eligibility_Requirements: Gross income of no more than 185 percent of the federal poverty level and at nutritional risk.

Program_Description: The WIC Program is committed to helping women understand the benefits to both mother and infant of breastfeeding their baby.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: The WIC program provides nutritional support and education to eligible women, infants, and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: WIC Breastfeeding Services provide support and resources for breastfeeding mothers.

WIC is a health and nutrition program for growing families that provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, vouchers for healthy foods, and referrals to healthcare and other community services.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children
Eligibility_Requirements: To find out if you are eligible for WIC services use the online eligibility assessment.
Application_Process: WIC appointments can be completed by phone or in-person at WIC sites.
